articles.headline: Iran dismisses U.S. bid to end conflict, sets own terms
articles.summary: Iran rejected a U.S. proposal for a ceasefire and sanctions relief, offering its own five conditions to end the war, which began with the assassination of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Tehran seeks a halt to aggression, reparations, prevention of future attacks, an end to fighting, and recognition of its sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: Washington, Baghdad, New York, Seoul: World News Briefs
articles.summary: Trump reschedules China visit for May amid West Asia conflict. New strike in Iraq kills seven security personnel. UN designates African slave trade 'gravest crime'. N. Korea vows stronger ties with Russia.

articles.headline: US to deploy 1,000 elite troops to West Asia amid Iran war effort
articles.summary: The US military is preparing to deploy at least 1,000 troops from the 82nd Airborne Division to West Asia amid escalating strikes and tensions. Thousands of Marines and rapid response naval vessels are also heading to the region.

articles.headline: Israel continues to pound Lebanon as Hezbollah presses on
articles.summary: Israel launched intensified strikes across southern Lebanon and Beirut's suburbs, targeting Hezbollah. Hezbollah said it continued attacks on Israeli troops, while Lebanon's Health Ministry reported over 1,000 deaths in the conflict.

articles.headline: Russia and Ukraine engage in tit-for-tat aerial barrages
articles.summary: Russia reported shooting down nearly 400 Ukrainian drones, while Ukraine claimed Russian drones and missiles hit civilian areas, killing at least six people. The escalation signals growing capabilities and potential offensives.

articles.headline: UN condemns Iran strikes on Gulf, demands ‘reparation’
articles.summary: The UN Human Rights Council condemned Iran's 'egregious attacks' on Gulf neighbors and demanded full reparation. The resolution, adopted by consensus, calls for Iran to cease all unprovoked attacks.

articles.headline: Iran rejects US ceasefire proposal and launches more attacks
articles.summary: Iran dismissed an American plan to pause the Middle East war, launching missile and drone attacks on Israel, Jordan, and Kuwait. An official stated Iran will only end the war on its own terms. Meanwhile, the US is deploying 1,000 paratroopers and 5,000 Marines to the region as tensions escalate following an assault that caused a fire at Kuwait International Airport.

articles.headline: Bangladesh PM terms 1971 genocide a pre-planned massacre
articles.summary: Bangladesh Prime Minister Tarique Rahman described the genocide of March 25, 1971, as a "pre-planned" massacre by Pakistani forces. Speaking on the anniversary of Operation Searchlight, he appealed for a society of equality and human dignity. Rahman stated that millions were killed during the Liberation War and emphasized the need to educate future generations about the tragedy.

articles.headline: India rejects Pakistan’s remarks on Asiya Andrabi’s life sentence
articles.summary: India dismissed Pakistan's condemnation of the life sentence given to Kashmiri separatist leader Asiya Andrabi. The Ministry of External Affairs stated Pakistan has no locus standi in India’s internal judicial processes. Andrabi, head of the banned Dukhtaran-e-Millat, was convicted under the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act for terrorist activities and inciting violence.

articles.headline: Iran allows safe passage for non-hostile vessels through Hormuz
articles.summary: Iran informed the International Maritime Organization that non-hostile vessels can transit the Strait of Hormuz if they coordinate with Iranian authorities. Passage remains restricted for ships linked to the US, Israel, or participants in "aggression" against Iran. Vessels must provide crew and cargo lists to the Revolutionary Guard to receive transit permission.

articles.headline: Centre defends stand as Opposition raises ‘Pak. role’ in West Asia
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar defended India's neutral stance in the West Asia conflict, calling Pakistan a 'dalal' for its mediating role. He stated India maintains a unique position, able to communicate with all sides, citing PM Modi's talks with Iranian authorities. India aims to protect its strategic and economic interests amid widespread Indian populations in the region.

articles.headline: Autorickshaw driver arrested for dumping woman’s body after hit-and-run
articles.summary: Kengeri police arrested an autorickshaw driver for dumping a woman's body behind a bus stop after hitting her with his vehicle. The accused, Basave Gowda, initially took her to a hospital but fled after realizing she had died from her injuries.

articles.headline: Plaque unveiled in memory of 11 people who lost their lives in stampede at RCB celebrations
articles.summary: Home Minister G. Parameshwara unveiled a plaque at the M. Chinnaswamy Stadium in memory of 11 lives lost in a stampede during RCB celebrations last year. KSCA and RCB were urged to take responsibility for safety in future events.

articles.headline: Man found carrying ₹6 lakh to Vidhana Soudha, questioned
articles.summary: Security personnel at Vidhana Soudha detained a man carrying ₹6 lakh in cash while attempting to enter the premises, raising security concerns. He was taken to the ACP's office for further questioning.

articles.headline: PI faces probe over sexual harassment allegation
articles.summary: City Police Commissioner ordered a preliminary inquiry against a police inspector accused of sexual harassment by a woman. The complainant alleged inappropriate messages and advances, with audio clips and messages surfacing on social media.

articles.headline: Office commute time nearly identical in Bengaluru and Hyderabad: Report
articles.summary: A report indicates that average one-way commute times in Bengaluru (59 mins) and Hyderabad (58 mins) are nearly identical, despite differences in infrastructure and city planning. Bengaluru's commute is shaped by congestion, while Hyderabad's has better traffic flow but longer distances.

articles.headline: Munnar estate worker dies in suspected elephant attack
articles.summary: A 62-year-old estate worker was found dead near Munnar in Kerala, suspected to be killed by a wild elephant. Forest officials are investigating, but conclusive evidence is pending. The victim had reportedly gone out to collect firewood.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh Police arrest three more terror suspects from various States
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh police arrested three more terror suspects from different states in Vijayawada, bringing the total arrests in the case to six. The suspects were apprehended based on reliable information.

articles.headline: 96% of Bastar region free from Maoism, says Chhattisgarh Dy. CM
articles.summary: Chhattisgarh Deputy Chief Minister Vijay Sharma stated that 96% of the Bastar region is free from Maoism, with only 30-40 cadres remaining. This comes after the surrender of Maoist commander Papa Rao and 17 others.

articles.headline: Two killed and 23 injured in Karol Bagh bus accident
articles.summary: A private sleeper bus traveling from Jaipur to Delhi overturned in the Karol Bagh area of central Delhi. Two men were killed and 23 others sustained injuries when the vehicle, carrying about 30 passengers, flipped while navigating a roundabout. The injured were transported to local hospitals for treatment following the early morning incident.

articles.headline: MP moves motion for rescue of Indian fishermen in Iran
articles.summary: Kanyakumari MP Vijay Vasanth moved an adjournment motion in the Lok Sabha to discuss the plight of over 1,000 fishermen from Tamil Nadu stranded in Iran. He reported that the individuals are facing acute food shortages and panic due to the ongoing conflict. Vasanth urged the government to initiate diplomatic measures for their immediate evacuation and medical assistance.

articles.headline: Canadian police arrest teenager for murder of Indian national Birinder Singh
articles.summary: An 18-year-old man was arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of 22-year-old Indian national Birinder Singh in Alberta, Canada. Singh had moved to Canada as a student three years ago. While some allege the attack was a racially motivated hate crime, investigators are still substantiating the motive. The suspect was traced to Saskatchewan and is being sent back to Alberta.

articles.headline: Govt. revamps UDAN scheme with changes in subsidy
articles.summary: The Centre extended the subsidy period for airlines on select Tier-2/3 routes from three to five years under the modified UDAN scheme, with a total outlay of ₹28,840 crore. Subsidies will shift from airfare levies to direct exchequer funding, aiming to revive routes that fell into disuse.

articles.headline: India said to have bought 7 mn barrels of crude LPG after U.S. eased sanctions
articles.summary: India has reportedly purchased its first cargo of Iranian LPG after the U.S. temporarily eased sanctions. The cargo, initially bound for China, will be shared among three Indian fuel retailers, with payment in rupees.

articles.headline: Paradip set to receive four tankers carrying over 7 million barrels of crude oil
articles.summary: Paradip Port is set to receive four tankers carrying over 7 million barrels of crude oil, exceeding India's average daily consumption. This addresses the ongoing gas crisis and aims to stabilize supplies.

articles.headline: ICICI Prudential gets I-T order for ₹391 crore
articles.summary: ICICI Prudential Life Insurance has received an Income Tax demand order for over ₹391 crore, including interest. The company plans to appeal the order before the appropriate authority.

articles.headline: Avantel bags ₹460-cr. contract for Railways
articles.summary: Avantel has secured a ₹459.9 crore contract from Zetwerk for the supply and AMC of Real-Time Train Information System (RTIS) devices for Indian Railways, enhancing operational efficiency.

articles.headline: It’s not a zero-one situation for 2-wheeler industry: Gupta
articles.summary: TVS Motor Company president Gaurav Gupta stated it's difficult to predict the West Asia crisis impact on the two-wheeler industry. He noted the company is focused on scenario planning and understanding the situation's evolving impact.

articles.headline: Indian tech start-ups raised $9.1 bn in 2025, 23% rise: Nasscom-Zinnov study
articles.summary: Indian tech startups raised $9.1 billion in 2025, a 23% increase, with deep-tech funding surging 37% driven by AI. Startups are entering a more disciplined growth phase, focusing on scalable ventures and AI commercialization.

articles.headline: Realtor Puravankara unveils 30 projects in South, Mumbai
articles.summary: Puravankara Ltd. plans to unveil 30 projects in South India and Mumbai over 24 months, comprising 51.14 million sq. ft. of developable area with a GDV of over ₹55,000 crore, marking a significant growth step.

articles.headline: What is at stake at the WTO’s MC14?
articles.summary: The WTO's MC14 in Cameroon faces challenges from geopolitical rivalry, trade unilateralism, and a weakened dispute settlement system. Key issues include plurilateral agreements, the e-commerce moratorium, and Special and Differential Treatment (SDT) for developing countries.

articles.headline: Google to verify investment and trading apps in India
articles.summary: Google will begin labeling verified investment apps on the Play Store in India to combat financial scams. SEBI Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey emphasized that fake apps pose a serious threat to investors and called for collaboration between tech firms and regulators. The initiative will ensure users can identify legitimate platforms via a verified badge.

articles.headline: India resumes Iranian LPG imports to secure energy supplies
articles.summary: India has purchased its first cargo of Iranian liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) since 2019 after the US temporarily eased sanctions. The cargo was bought in rupees to mitigate supply disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz caused by the West Asia war. While the government aims to diversify sources, the move reflects the urgent need to secure cooking gas supplies for the domestic market.

articles.headline: India raises clean-energy ambition with 60% non-fossil fuel power goal by 2035
articles.summary: India pledged that by 2035, 60% of its installed electric capacity will comprise non-fossil sources. The country also aims to reduce emission intensity by 47% per GDP unit from 2005 levels and increase its carbon sink to 3.5 billion tonnes, updating its Nationally Determined Contribution (NDC).

articles.headline: Cauvery basin to face dry spell until 2050, says study
articles.summary: A study suggests the Cauvery basin may face a 3.5% decline in water by 2050, unlike northern rivers expected to face floods. Researchers warn that water sharing could become tougher, citing historical disputes and failed negotiations.

articles.headline: An energy transition driven by ethics
articles.summary: The article argues that dependence on fossil fuels hinders national security and sovereignty. It highlights critical mineral supply chain bottlenecks and the need for ethical considerations in energy transition, rather than just economic benefits.

articles.headline: West Asia conflict: how finding oil changed the Persian Gulf ’s ecology
articles.summary: The article explores the ecological transformation of the Persian Gulf due to oil exploration and geopolitical tensions. Increased urbanization, pollution, and industrial activities have severely impacted its fragile ecosystems, including mangroves, coral reefs, and dugong populations.

articles.headline: How do human activities contribute to Chennai’s microplastic problem?
articles.summary: Research shows Chennai's beach sediments have microplastics, primarily nylon fibres, linked to fishing, synthetic clothes, tourism, and sewage. These pose ecological risks to marine life and potentially human health via contaminated seafood.

articles.headline: India approves enhanced climate targets for 2031-2035 period
articles.summary: The Union cabinet approved revised climate goals as part of its Nationally Determined Contribution. India aims to reduce the emissions intensity of its GDP by 47% by 2035 from 2005 levels. Other targets include drawing 60% of installed electricity capacity from non-fossil sources and increasing carbon sinks to absorb up to 4 billion tonnes of CO2 equivalent by 2035.

articles.headline: Meet on Deendayal Upadhyaya begins despite cancellation order
articles.summary: A three-day conference on Deendayal Upadhyaya commenced at Karnataka State Open University (KSOU) in Mysuru despite official directives to cancel it due to protocol violations. KSOU accounts have also been frozen by the government.

articles.headline: Zubeen Garg’s death ruled accidental drowning by Singapore court
articles.summary: A coroner’s court in Singapore ruled that the death of Assam singer Zubeen Garg was an accidental drowning with no evidence of foul play. The court found high levels of alcohol in his blood but no indication of coercion. The findings contradict conspiracy claims made by the singer's family and supporters following the incident during a cultural festival in Singapore.

articles.headline: Bengali writer Ramkumar Mukhopadhyay selected for Saraswati Samman
articles.summary: Eminent Bengali novelist Ramkumar Mukhopadhyay has been selected for the prestigious 35th Saraswati Samman (2025) for his work 'Hara Parbati Katha'. The novel reimagines the story of Parvati and Shiva through a modern lens. The award, instituted by the KK Birla Foundation, includes a ₹15 lakh prize. Mukhopadhyay has authored numerous novels and short story collections.

articles.headline: City corporations to present budgets on March 27, 28
articles.summary: Bengaluru's five city corporations will present their budgets on March 27 and 28, with a total outlay expected to exceed ₹21,000 crore. The State government will provide financial assistance to corporations facing budget deficits.

articles.headline: BMRCL extends metro services till 2 a.m. for IPL match at Chinnaswamy stadium on March 28
articles.summary: Bengaluru Metro Rail Corporation Ltd. (BMRCL) will extend services until 2 a.m. on March 28 for the TATA IPL T20 match. Last trains from terminal stations will depart later, with extra parking facilities provided at metro stations to ease traffic congestion.

articles.headline: Toll charges in Karnataka to rise from April 1
articles.summary: Vehicle users in Karnataka will likely face increased toll charges from April 1 due to NHAI's annual revision linked to WPI. The hike is expected to be moderate, around 5%, and will apply to most toll plazas, with some seeing the increase later.

articles.headline: MoPNG issues directions to strengthen natural gas infra
articles.summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas has introduced reforms to accelerate piped natural gas network expansion and improve last-mile connectivity. Reforms include faster approvals for laying pipelines and investor-friendly networks.

articles.headline: Government approves ₹28,840 crore for revamped UDAN air scheme
articles.summary: The Union cabinet approved a revamped UDAN regional air connectivity scheme with an outlay of ₹28,840 crore for 10 years. The plan includes developing 100 new airports and 200 modern helipads. It provides viability gap funding to airlines to operate routes in underserved and remote regions, targeting 40 million passengers over the next decade.

articles.headline: Ghaziabad administration prepares for Noida International Airport inauguration
articles.summary: The Ghaziabad administration has requisitioned 800 buses to transport people to the inauguration of the Noida International Airport on March 28. To manage the expected crowds and traffic, authorities have also ordered schools in the district to conduct online classes. The airport is a major infrastructure project for the National Capital Region.

articles.headline: Bengaluru waste collection resumes following dispute over segregation rules
articles.summary: Garbage collection in Bengaluru returned to normal after a day of disruption caused by a conflict between contractors and civic authorities. Contractors had resisted new directives regarding manual waste segregation. While services resumed, the administration registered several FIRs against contractors for neglect of duty under the Essential Services Maintenance Act.

articles.headline: Andhra CM sets target of 50,000 new hotel rooms
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu directed officials to ensure the construction of 50,000 hotel rooms by 2029 to boost tourism. The plan includes 10,000 homestay facilities and new amusement parks in Visakhapatnam and Tirupati. Naidu called for the acceleration of projects approved by the State Investment Promotion Board to enhance hospitality infrastructure.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh requests central approval for Polavaram project designs
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh Irrigation Minister Nimmala Ramanaidu met with central officials to request immediate approval for pending designs of the Polavaram irrigation project. The state is seeking reimbursement for canal work and support for river embankment strengthening. A high-level team from the Jal Shakti ministry inspected the site following concerns flagged by foreign experts.

articles.headline: India’s first repository of major psychiatric disorders launched in Bengaluru
articles.summary: CALM-Brain, India's first digital repository of data on brain structure and function from psychiatric disorders, has been launched in Bengaluru. Built on Indian patient data, it will be open-source for clinicians and researchers to study neuropsychiatric disorders.

articles.headline: Antiprotons take a ride in novel test by CERN scientists
articles.summary: CERN scientists successfully transported antiprotons in a specially designed trap on a truck for a road test. This experiment aims to compare matter and antimatter, seeking differences by testing the trap's ability to contain antiprotons during transport.

articles.headline: What is mineral water and how does it naturally contain dissolved minerals?
articles.summary: Mineral water originates from protected underground reservoirs, acquiring natural minerals over time. Unlike treated tap water, it retains its unique mineral composition, which varies by region and affects taste and bodily functions.

articles.headline: Study finds world’s oldest dog DNA in modern-day Turkey
articles.summary: New research suggests the bond between humans and dogs dates back nearly 16,000 years, which is 5,000 years earlier than previously believed. The findings are based on the discovery of the world's oldest canine DNA in Pinarbasi, Turkey. The study provides fresh insights into the domestication of dogs and their long-standing relationship with human civilizations across the globe.

articles.headline: HC: Venues serving liquor must initiate rigorous age verification protocols
articles.summary: The High Court of Karnataka ruled that establishments serving liquor must implement strict age verification, like Aadhaar checks, and re-verify when liquor is ordered by youthful individuals. Managements will be held accountable for minors gaining entry and consuming alcohol.

articles.headline: One Bill to reintroduce ballot papers in local body polls passed amid walkout
articles.summary: A Bill to reintroduce ballot papers in local body polls was passed in the Karnataka Legislative Council amid an Opposition walkout. The Bill also proposes stricter measures against electoral malpractices and aims to strengthen local governance by taxing un-authorized properties.

articles.headline: DC summoned over delay in providing office space to MLC
articles.summary: Legislative Council Chairman Basavaraj Horatti summoned the Bidar Deputy Commissioner over the failure to provide office space to MLC Bhimrao Patil for four years, calling it an affront to the House's dignity.

articles.headline: The Transgender Persons Amendment Bill, a flawed fix
articles.summary: The Transgender Persons Amendment Bill 2026 is criticized for narrowing definitions, conflating sex and gender identity, ignoring intersex rights, and perpetuating colonial structures like the hijra jamath-gharana system. It also lacks intersectional analysis and fails to grant civil/marriage rights.

articles.headline: Vande Mataram advisory ‘not a threat to conform’
articles.summary: The Supreme Court stated the advisory on playing the National Song Vande Mataram is not a threat to conform and does not violate freedoms. The court noted that while individual conscience is respected, refusal to sing or stand carries a burden. It termed the petition challenging the advisory as premature.

articles.headline: Bill strikes dustbin on gender rights amid uproar
articles.summary: The Rajya Sabha passed the Transgender Persons Amendment Bill 2026 amid uproar, with two NCTP members resigning citing it as a step backward. The Bill removes self-determination of gender and mandates medical boards for identification, drawing criticism for discrimination.

articles.headline: Supreme Court warns litigant for trying to influence judicial proceedings
articles.summary: Chief Justice Surya Kant took exception to an attempt to improperly influence the court after a litigant's father allegedly called the CJI's brother to question a court order. The CJI warned of possible criminal contempt action, stating that judicial decisions cannot be dictated by outside parties. The case involved a petitioner seeking minority benefits through religious conversion.

articles.headline: UK High Court refuses to reopen Nirav Modi extradition case
articles.summary: The High Court of Justice in London rejected fugitive Nirav Modi's bid to reopen his extradition case. The ruling accepted assurances from the Indian government that Modi would not be interrogated by specific agencies and would receive proper medical care. This decision appears to exhaust Modi's legal options in the UK, clearing the path for his return to India after seven years.

articles.headline: Supreme Court forms SIT to probe minor's assault in Gurugram
articles.summary: The Supreme Court established a Special Investigation Team to probe the alleged sexual assault of a four-year-old girl in Gurugram. The court expressed shock at the insensitivity of the local police during the initial investigation and the trauma caused to the victim. The SIT was mandated to ensure a sensitive and professional investigation into the crime.

articles.headline: Allahabad High Court grants anticipatory bail to Swami Avimukteshwaranand
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court granted anticipatory bail to Swami Avimukteshwaranand Saraswati and a disciple in a case registered under the POCSO Act. The case involved allegations of sexual exploitation of minor disciples. The Swami has previously been in the news for public confrontations with the Uttar Pradesh government regarding his treatment during the Magh Mela.

articles.headline: Karnataka legislature passes bill to prevent crimes in name of honor
articles.summary: The Karnataka Legislative Council passed the Freedom of Choice in Marriage Bill, aimed at preventing violence and honor killings. The legislation makes family or clan consent unnecessary once two adults agree to marry. It introduces a minimum five-year prison sentence for honor killings and mandates police protection for threatened couples within six hours of a complaint.

articles.headline: Autorickshaw driver arrested for fatal hit-and-run in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Bengaluru police arrested a 50-year-old autorickshaw driver for a fatal hit-and-run incident. After striking a 50-year-old woman near Kengeri, the driver allegedly attempted to take her to a hospital but dumped her body in a heap of garbage after she died. The suspect was traced using CCTV footage and technical evidence. The victim was returning from her hometown.

articles.headline: High Court quashes land encroachment FIR against Ravi Shankar
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court set aside an FIR against Art of Living founder Ravi Shankar concerning alleged encroachment of government land in Bengaluru South. The court observed that there were no specific allegations against him in the complaint. The FIR had been registered following a public interest litigation seeking the demolition of structures on the disputed land.

articles.headline: AAP MLA Harmeet Singh Pathanmajra arrested in rape case
articles.summary: Punjab Police arrested AAP MLA Harmeet Singh Pathanmajra from Gwalior, Madhya Pradesh, after he spent six months on the run. Pathanmajra faces charges of rape, cheating, and criminal intimidation following a complaint by a woman who alleged he misrepresented his marital status. The MLA had been declared a proclaimed offender and was caught despite using multiple mobile numbers.

articles.headline: Supreme Court rules Vande Mataram protocol is advisory
articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a petition challenging the government's circular on the national song Vande Mataram, clarifying it is "advisory in nature." The court ruled there is no legal penalty for choosing not to sing the song. The bench stated that the circular merely sets protocol for respect and decorum, and judicial intervention is not required unless coercive action occur.

articles.headline: Government introduces FCRA amendment to manage assets of cancelled NGOs
articles.summary: The Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill, 2026, was introduced in the Lok Sabha, granting the government powers to manage assets created with foreign funds by NGOs whose licenses are cancelled. The bill aims to prevent the misuse of foreign funding for activities detrimental to national interest. It also proposes fixing timelines for the utilization of foreign funds.

articles.headline: Rajya Sabha clears Transgender Persons Amendment Bill
articles.summary: The Rajya Sabha passed the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill 2026 by voice vote. Minister Virendra Kumar stated the bill ensures dignity for the community. However, opposition members and activists expressed concern that the bill's narrow definitions violate constitutional rights to self-determination and privacy, and pleaded for wider community consultation.

articles.headline: Supreme Court upholds retrospective compensation for highway land acquisition
articles.summary: The Supreme Court refused to review its ruling that grants solatium and interest to landowners whose property was acquired for national highways between 1997 and 2015. Despite the NHAI citing a potential ₹29,000-crore liability, the court held that fiscal considerations cannot override the constitutional guarantee of just compensation for land-losers.

articles.headline: ‘AI is not just a tech wave, it is a reordering of global capability’
articles.summary: M.R. Rangaswami stated that AI is reshaping every sector, with Indian startups collectively raising $3.6 billion. He highlighted India's advantage in the AI era and the reordering of global capability driven by Indian talent. AI is seen as transforming industry and innovation.

articles.headline: US judge questions govt.’s motives for labelling Anthropic as a security threat
articles.summary: A federal judge questioned the government's motives for designating AI company Anthropic a national security risk. The judge expressed skepticism, suggesting it could be retaliation for Anthropic criticizing government contracting positions.

articles.headline: California jury finds social media platforms liable for child addiction
articles.summary: In a first-of-its-kind ruling, a California jury held Instagram and YouTube responsible for design features that addicted a young user, awarding the plaintiff $3 million. The lawsuit claimed the platforms ignored mental health risks. The verdict could influence thousands of similar lawsuits filed against social media companies regarding their impact on the well-being of minors.

articles.headline: Union cabinet approves ₹1,800 crore for IVFRT immigration project
articles.summary: The Union cabinet approved ₹1,800 crore for the continuation of the Immigration, Visa, Foreigners Registration & Tracking (IVFRT) project for five years. The project integrates functions related to visa issuance and the registration of foreigners to facilitate legitimate travel while strengthening national security. It maintains a database of arrivals and hotel stays across India.

articles.headline: Donald Trump appoints tech leaders to new presidential AI council
articles.summary: Donald Trump has established a Council of Advisors on Science and Technology to focus on artificial intelligence policy. The council includes industry leaders such as Mark Zuckerberg, Larry Ellison, and Jensen Huang. Chaired by venture capitalist David Sacks, the group will advise the administration on science-related issues and the development of AI frameworks for the United States.

articles.headline: System of apprenticeship has ‘completely changed’
articles.summary: T.K. Kurien noted that the apprenticeship system has fundamentally changed, with professionals needing to understand the 'why' of operations, not just the 'what'. He urged educational systems to adapt to this shift towards co-creation and real-world complexity.

articles.headline: Indian universities double presence in global top 50 subject rankings
articles.summary: The number of Indian higher education institutions ranked in the top 50 globally for specific subjects rose to 27 in the 2026 QS World University Rankings. IIT Bombay and IIT Delhi broke into the top 50 for computer science. While overall participation increased, some premier institutions saw slight rank drops in broad engineering categories due to rising global competition.

articles.headline: ‘State better placed than its neighbours in fiscal deficit’
articles.summary: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah claimed Karnataka's fiscal deficit is within limits and better than neighbouring states. He blamed Centre's policies for revenue deficits and stated loans are utilized for public welfare. Debt is high in developed economies, he noted.

articles.headline: Banter over CM’s shawl presented at ‘auspicious’ time
articles.summary: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah's shawl choice and presenting the budget at an 'auspicious' time sparked a light-hearted exchange in the Assembly. He defended his choices and invoked social reformer Basavanna, drawing laughter.

articles.headline: Mahadayi, Upper Krishna projects raise debate
articles.summary: The Mahadayi drinking water project triggered a debate between CM Siddaramaiah and LoP Arvind Bellad. Bellad questioned the Congress's commitment to Mahadayi, while Siddaramaiah accused the BJP of stalling the project. Both sides traded barbs over fiscal deficit and project completion.

articles.headline: Govt. furnishes 96% replies after eviction of encroachers deadline
articles.summary: The government provided responses to 96% of the 1,573 questions raised during the Budget session before the Chairman's deadline, improving compliance. Chairman Basavaraj Horatti had warned of action against officials for delays.

articles.headline: Democracy, dissent and the ‘national image’ debate
articles.summary: The article discusses the tension between dissent and the state in democracies, arguing that protests demonstrating democratic credentials can be misconstrued as anti-national. It highlights how the government and mass media often blur the lines between criticism of policy and disloyalty to the nation.

articles.headline: Tidings from Kejriwal’s comeback
articles.summary: The article discusses Arvind Kejriwal's return to Delhi's political scene after his discharge from a liquor policy case. It highlights the challenges for the BJP government in Delhi and the potential impact of Kejriwal's reappearance on state politics.

articles.headline: Rural Telangana’s verdict on the Congress, BRS and BJP
articles.summary: The analysis of Telangana's rural local body elections shows the Congress winning 53.7% of gram panchayats, BRS 27.7%, and BJP 5.5%. MLA defections significantly impacted BRS performance, while the Congress showed strength in tribal and Dalit seats.

articles.headline: Cong. gets notice to vacate its iconic 24 Akbar Road office
articles.summary: The Congress has been issued a notice to vacate its 24 Akbar Road office by March 28. The party is considering its strategy and legal options, while sources indicate it may not vacate the premises.

articles.headline: Congress questions Modi govt.’s ‘U-turn’ on women’s quota law
articles.summary: The Congress accused the Modi government of a U-turn on the women's reservation law, demanding immediate implementation after delimitation and census. They criticized the move as a 'weapon of mass diversion' from policy failures.

articles.headline: FM defends Centre’s right to levy cess, says Constitution allows it
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman reiterated the Centre's constitutional right to levy cesses and surcharges, stating that over the last six years, the Centre spent 105% of its collections on States. She rejected claims of unfair levies.

articles.headline: Seven CAPF personnel removed from poll duty for attending Iftar
articles.summary: The Election Commission removed seven Central Armed Police Forces personnel from election duty in West Bengal. The action was taken after they allegedly attended an Iftar gathering hosted by a local politician in Murshidabad district, which violated neutrality guidelines. The incident came to light after photographs of the event were circulated on social media.

articles.headline: AIADMK releases first list of 23 candidates for Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: The AIADMK announced its first list of 23 candidates for the Tamil Nadu assembly elections. General Secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami will contest from Edappadi. The party will contest 169 seats, while its NDA allies will contest 65. The party's manifesto promises monthly allowances for women and free cooking gas cylinders to offset rising costs.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi alleges secret partnership between BJP and LDF
articles.summary: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi alleged that the lack of progress in corruption cases against Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan proves collusion between the CPI(M) and the BJP. Addressing a rally via video link, Gandhi questioned why the BJP attacks him with central agencies but ignores the Kerala CM. He claimed the UDF is the only coalition offering a real challenge to the BJP-LDF bond.

articles.headline: Telangana CM warns against proposed seat increase through delimitation
articles.summary: Telangana Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy expressed strong opposition to a proposal to increase Lok Sabha and assembly seats by 50%. He argued the move is a conspiracy that would weaken the political relevance of South India while disproportionately benefiting northern states. Reddy called for a fair regional balance to prevent political discrimination against the south.

articles.headline: Senior leader T Jeevan Reddy resigns from Telangana Congress
articles.summary: Veteran leader T Jeevan Reddy resigned from the primary membership of the Congress party and the AICC executive committee. He cited the encouragement of defectors and the neglect of senior leaders by the state leadership as reasons for his departure. Reddy expressed frustration over the induction of rival MLA Dr. M Sanjay Kumar, ending his 42-year association with the party.

articles.headline: Over one million names deleted from West Bengal electoral rolls
articles.summary: Election Commission officials confirmed that approximately 35% to 40% of the 3.2 million cases of voter discrepancies in West Bengal were deleted from the rolls. The ruling TMC and opposition BJP exchanged sharp attacks over the deletion, with the BJP claiming millions of "fake" voters were removed while the TMC alleged technical glitches and hacking of the EC website.

articles.headline: BJP fields RG Kar victim’s mother in Panihati seat
articles.summary: The BJP released its third list of 19 candidates for the West Bengal assembly elections, featuring the mother of the trainee doctor murdered at RG Kar Hospital. The candidate will contest the Panihati seat to campaign for women's safety. The list also includes a retired NSG commando. The BJP has now named candidates for 274 of the 294 assembly seats.

articles.headline: Bill to formalize IPS deputation in CAPFs introduced in Rajya Sabha
articles.summary: The government introduced the Central Armed Police Forces (General Administration) Bill, 2026, to create a unified legal framework for CAPFs. The bill reserves senior leadership positions, including Director General posts, for IPS officers on deputation. Opposition members and CAPF cadre officers have opposed the move, citing career stagnation for departmental officers.

articles.headline: Medical student found dead at hostel in Bidar
articles.summary: A final-year MBBS student was found dead at his hostel in Bidar, suspected to have died by suicide after reportedly losing ₹80,000 in online gaming. Police are investigating the incident.

articles.headline: Centre asks RBI to keep retail inflation target at 4% till 2031
articles.summary: The Union government has asked the RBI to target retail inflation at 4% with a 2% margin until March 31, 2031. This move aims to control price rise, continuing the mandate given in 2016.

articles.headline: Households must switch to PNG or face LPG supply discontinuation
articles.summary: The government has ordered households in areas with existing piped natural gas (PNG) infrastructure to switch from LPG cylinders within three months or face cessation of gas refill supplies. The move aims to reduce India's 60% import dependency on LPG, which has become critical due to supply disruptions and conflict in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: Lok Sabha passes Finance Bill with amendments on share buybacks
articles.summary: The Lok Sabha passed the Finance Bill 2026, which includes a flat 12% surcharge on share buybacks. The bill treats buyback proceeds as capital gains rather than dividends. Amendments also increased the turnover limit for startup tax holidays to ₹300 crore and introduced measures to improve the efficiency of income tax administration and reduce litigation.

articles.headline: Karnataka CM defends budget and fiscal management in Assembly
articles.summary: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah defended Karnataka's 2026-27 budget, stating the state's fiscal deficit of 2.95% remains within acceptable limits. He dismissed opposition criticism regarding revenue projections and the timing of the budget presentation. The CM also noted his commitment to rational thinking and stated he would present more budgets if allowed by party leadership.

articles.headline: Karnataka government urges public to avoid panic buying of fuel
articles.summary: The Karnataka government issued a statement assuring the public that there is no shortage of petrol or diesel. The advisory follows reports of long queues at fuel stations due to rumors. Authorities have prohibited the sale of fuel in loose containers like bottles due to safety risks and have instructed outlets to supply fuel only directly to vehicle tanks.

articles.headline: Fuel stations shut in Hyderabad and Telangana amid panic buying
articles.summary: Several petrol pumps in Hyderabad and other parts of Telangana were forced to shut down after depleting stocks due to panic buying. Motorists rushed to fill tanks following rumors of an impending fuel shortage. Authorities clarified that supply chains are robust, but the sudden surge in demand led to temporary "no stock" situations at many retail outlets.

articles.headline: ED attaches ₹1,700 crore in assets linked to Mahadev betting app
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate attached 20 properties worth ₹1,700 crore in Dubai and New Delhi belonging to Mahadev betting app promoter Sourabh Chandrakar. Attached assets include luxury villas in Dubai and apartments in the Burj Khalifa. The move is part of a money laundering investigation into an estimated ₹6,000 crore illegal betting empire masterminded from abroad.

articles.headline: S&P Global raises India’s GDP growth forecast for 2026-27
articles.summary: S&P Global upgraded India’s GDP growth forecast for the 2026-27 financial year to 7.1%, up from 6.7%. The agency cited resilient private consumption and solid exports as key drivers. However, it warned that global conflicts and high energy prices remain threats that could strain fiscal positions and depress domestic demand if crude oil prices stay elevated over the long term.

articles.headline: India to push for permanent food security solution at WTO
articles.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal will head the Indian delegation at the 14th WTO ministerial conference. India will advocate for a permanent solution on public stockholding for food security to protect its minimum support price system. Other priorities include ensuring fair fisheries subsidies and restoring a functional dispute settlement mechanism within the global trade body.

articles.headline: India prepares $1 billion tender for Odisha crude reserve
articles.summary: Amid West Asia supply concerns, India is set to float a $1 billion tender for a 4-million-tonne strategic crude oil reserve facility in Chandikhol, Odisha. Another project in Padur, Karnataka, is also moving forward. These strategic reserves aim to ensure energy security for the country, which currently imports about 90% of its total oil requirements to meet domestic demand.

articles.headline: Bhatia, Molinari in focus as Indian Open tees off at the tough DLF course
articles.summary: Akshay Bhatia and Francesco Molinari are among the top players at the Hero Indian Open. Bhatia, seeking to conquer the challenging DLF course, aims for more wins, while Molinari hopes to revive his career. Defending champion Chacarra and others are also in contention.

articles.headline: MI banks on a galaxy of stars to end drought
articles.summary: Mumbai Indians, seeking its first IPL title since 2020, boasts a strong squad with experienced players and new talent. The key lies in finding the right combination early to build momentum and shed its reputation as a slow starter.

articles.headline: I’ve made mistakes, but that’s history: Shaw on his journey
articles.summary: Prithvi Shaw reflects on his career's ups and downs, admitting mistakes but focusing on the present. He credits a break for enhancing his mental toughness and is working hard for a comeback to the Indian team.

articles.headline: Pace is ace: Gurindervir breaks national record in 60m
articles.summary: Gurindervir Singh broke the national record in the men's 60m dash with a time of 6.60 seconds at the National Indoor Athletics Championships. Harita Bhandra won the women's 60m dash.

articles.headline: Boxing coach Nieva believes team-building activities can make a big difference
articles.summary: Indian boxing coach Santiago Nieva is engaging his team in activities like photo contests and visiting historical sites to foster camaraderie and boost performance. He believes team bonding is crucial for boxers' careers and overall well-being.

articles.headline: When the technique is right, performance follows: record-breaker Baranica’s mantra
articles.summary: Pole vaulter Baranica Elangovan broke the national record and achieved a personal best with 4.22m. She attributes her success to focusing on technique rather than just performance, emphasizing consistency and refinement.

articles.headline: ODI WC crown gives us the confidence to win anywhere: Harmanpreet
articles.summary: Captain Harmanpreet Kaur believes the 2025 ODI World Cup victory instilled confidence for the team to win in any format or location. She highlighted the recent multi-format series in Australia as a significant learning experience.

articles.headline: Time for Australian cricket to do some soul-searching
articles.summary: Former coach John Buchanan suggests Australian cricket needs soul-searching, particularly regarding its domestic system and nurturing young talent. He notes the decline in skill levels and the need for accountability across good and bad outcomes.

articles.headline: Mohamed Salah announces departure from Liverpool at end of season
articles.summary: Egyptian forward Mohamed Salah confirmed he will leave Liverpool at the end of the current season, ending a decorated career at the club. Salah won multiple titles, including the Premier League and Champions League. His departure follows a season marked by injury and a reported rift with the club management. He leaves as one of the club's all-time top goalscorers.

articles.headline: Hero Indian Open golf tournament begins at Gurugram course
articles.summary: The Hero Indian Open golf tournament starts at the DLF Golf and Country Club, with players warning of a challenging Gary Player-designed course. Defending champion Eugenio Chacarra noted that rising temperatures are making the greens firmer. Top-ranked Akshay Bhatia is a key attraction in the 138-player field, which includes several Major winners and top Indian professional talent.

articles.headline: Outgoing women’s football coach criticizes training schedule and support
articles.summary: In her final report to the AIFF, Indian women's football coach Amelia Valverde stated that an excessive number of friendly matches and a lack of support staff hindered training. India was eliminated from the AFC Women's Asian Cup after losing all group matches. Valverde recommended better psychological tools for players and utilizing official FIFA windows to play national teams.

articles.headline: IPL captains discuss Impact Player rule and new ball protocol
articles.summary: During a meeting at BCCI headquarters, IPL captains expressed varied views on the Impact Player rule, which some believe diminishes the role of all-rounders. Officials also appraised captains on a new protocol allowing bowling teams to request a ball replacement once after the 10th over in evening games to manage dew. The 2026 season will maintain existing playing conditions.

articles.headline: Prithvi Shaw focuses on IPL comeback with Delhi Capitals
articles.summary: After a period of poor form and off-field scrutiny, Prithvi Shaw expressed a renewed focus on his performance for Delhi Capitals. Shaw stated he is no longer setting steep targets but is focusing on finding joy in the game. After working hard on his fitness and technique during his time away, the 26-year-old aims for a successful season to facilitate an India team comeback.

articles.headline: Sprinter Gurindervir Singh sets new national 60m record
articles.summary: Sprinter Gurindervir Singh broke the Indian national 60-meter record at the National Indoor Championships in Bhubaneswar. Singh clocked a time of 6.60 seconds, surpassing the previous record of 6.67 seconds set by Elakiya Dasan in 2018. The feat marks a significant achievement in Indian athletics, though teammate Animesh Kujur was disqualified in the same event.
